summary: On UWP, having multiple SKGLViews on a page and removing one of them causes a crash when the remaining view next redraws (e.g., on window resize).

stepsToReproduce: Add two SKGLViews to a UWP/Xamarin.Forms page
Trigger a redraw on the first SKGLView (e.g., resize the window)

repoLinks.description: Mapsui issue where the crash was first discovered
Related: AccessViolationException in UWP when navigating with DrawInBackground=true

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The AngleSwapChainPanel/GlesContext code still uses the same static eglDisplay pattern. No fix has been committed for this scenario.

analysis.summary: Crash caused by a shared static EGLDisplay in GlesContext: all AngleSwapChainPanel instances (one per SKGLView) share the same EGL display, creating a race condition when one context is disposed (via GC finalizer or Unloaded event) while the other's render loop is still active.
analysis.rationale: The reporter provides a complete repro project and clear steps. The crash occurs only on UWP/ANGLE (Android and iOS are unaffected), pointing to the Windows-specific GlesContext implementation. Code inspection confirms that GlesContext.eglDisplay is static — all instances share it. When the second view is removed (OnUnloaded → Dispose), its EGL context is destroyed via the shared display. If the first view's render loop or the GC finalizer runs concurrently, ANGLE's D3D11 state machine encounters unsynchronized EGL calls, resulting in a memory access violation.

keySignals.text: removing one will cause a crash
keySignals.interpretation: Crash is tied to disposal of the second SKGLView while the first is still active.

keySignals.text: I tested this on Android and iOS without issue, it appears to only be within the UWP project
keySignals.interpretation: Platform-specific — implicates the UWP ANGLE/EGL path exclusively.

keySignals.text: Just going to mention that I tried the latest 2.80.1 and this is still a problem
keySignals.interpretation: Not fixed in 2.80.1 — persists across at least two minor releases.

keySignals.text: I think this could be the same problem as #1490 (or at least the underlying cause could be the same)
keySignals.interpretation: A contributor explicitly links this to #1490, pointing to a shared root cause in GlesContext lifecycle.

keySignals.text: The only trigger at the moment is when the form is Garbage collected
keySignals.interpretation: GC finalizer path confirms GlesContext.~GlesContext() is a trigger, consistent with the threading race.

codeInvestigation.finding: eglDisplay is declared as 'private static EGLDisplay eglDisplay = Egl.EGL_NO_DISPLAY'. This shared static field means ALL GlesContext instances across all AngleSwapChainPanel instances use the same EGL display object.

~GlesContext() finalizer calls Dispose(false), which calls DestroySurface() then Cleanup(). Cleanup() calls eglDestroyContext(eglDisplay, eglContext). The GC finalizer thread can run this concurrently while the render loop thread calls eglMakeCurrent(eglDisplay, ...) on the remaining view, causing unsynchronized EGL calls on the shared display.

OnUnloaded calls UpdateRenderLoop(false) to cancel the render loop, then DestroyRenderSurface(), then glesContext.Dispose(). However, renderLoopWorker.Cancel() is not awaited — the background render thread may still be executing RenderFrame() when Dispose() is called on the context.

RenderFrame() checks glesContext?.HasSurface but does not acquire any lock. The glesContext reference and its EGL state can be accessed from the background render thread simultaneously with OnUnloaded's disposal path on the UI thread.

nextQuestions: Does the crash occur even with DrawInBackground=false (synchronous rendering)?
Does using SKCanvasView (raster/CPU backend) instead of SKGLView avoid the crash?
Is the eglDisplay static field intentional for sharing ANGLE's D3D device, or an oversight?
workarounds: Use SKCanvasView instead of SKGLView where multiple views need to be dynamically added or removed — avoids ANGLE/EGL context management entirely.
Avoid placing multiple SKGLViews on the same page in UWP; use a single SKGLView and swap content programmatically.
Ensure the second SKGLView is fully removed and GC'd (call GC.Collect() after removal) before triggering a redraw on the first view — reduces (but does not eliminate) the race window.

resolution.hypothesis: The static eglDisplay in GlesContext causes concurrent EGL access when multiple views are active. Fix requires either making eglDisplay instance-scoped (separate ANGLE D3D devices per view) or adding synchronization around all EGL calls that touch the shared display.

proposals.title: Use SKCanvasView instead of SKGLView (workaround)
proposals.description: Replace SKGLView with SKCanvasView in scenarios where views are dynamically added/removed. SKCanvasView uses the raster (CPU) backend and avoids ANGLE/EGL entirely.

proposals.title: Make eglDisplay instance-scoped
proposals.description: Remove the 'static' keyword from eglDisplay in GlesContext and initialize a separate EGL display per instance. Each view gets its own ANGLE D3D device, eliminating the shared-state race. Trade-off: slightly higher D3D resource usage per view.

proposals.title: Add synchronization around EGL calls
proposals.description: Introduce a static lock object in GlesContext and acquire it around all EGL API calls (eglMakeCurrent, eglDestroyContext, eglDestroySurface, eglCreateWindowSurface). This serializes EGL access across all instances sharing the same display.

resolution.recommendedReason: Immediately actionable for the reporter. The proper fix (instance-scoped display or EGL synchronization) requires deeper investigation to avoid breaking existing ANGLE rendering behavior on UWP.

actionability.reason: Real crash with complete repro project. Related to #1490 (same root cause suspected). Needs investigation to confirm whether making eglDisplay instance-scoped or adding EGL synchronization is the correct fix.

actions.description: Add missing area/SkiaSharp.Views and tenet/reliability labels
Cross-reference issue #1490 which has the same underlying GlesContext lifecycle root cause
Acknowledge the report, provide workaround (SKCanvasView), and explain the root cause hypothesis
actions.comment:
Thanks for the detailed repro! This looks like a threading/lifecycle bug in the UWP `GlesContext` implementation.

**Root cause hypothesis:** `GlesContext.eglDisplay` is a `static` field shared by all `AngleSwapChainPanel` (SKGLView) instances. When the second view is removed, its EGL context is destroyed via the shared display. If the GC finalizer (running on a GC thread) or the `Unloaded` event handler races with the first view's render loop, ANGLE's D3D11 state machine gets concurrent EGL calls on the shared display — causing the crash. This is likely the same underlying issue as #1490.

**Immediate workaround:** Replace `SKGLView` with `SKCanvasView` if your scenario involves dynamically adding/removing views. `SKCanvasView` uses the CPU raster backend and avoids ANGLE/EGL entirely.

We'll investigate whether making `eglDisplay` instance-scoped or adding synchronization around EGL calls is the right fix.
